'''Justin Sangaré''' (born 7 March 1998) is a [[France national rugby league team|France international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]].

He is a product of the Toulouse academy system.

==Background==
Sangaré was born in Mali but grew up in the Bagatelle district of Toulouse and discovered [[rugby league]] aged 10 when the Toulouse Olympique community programme visited his school.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://everythingrugbyleague.com/justin-sangare-toulouse-and-france-local-hero/|title=Justin SANGARÉ Toulouse and France local hero|website=www.everythingrugbyleague.com|date=April 19, 2020}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Toulouse Olympique Broncos===
Sangaré graduated from Toulouse's "Centre de Formation" and made his debut for the Toulouse reserve side on 19 September 2015 coming off the bench in the 16–24 defeat to  win at home to [[Saint-Estève XIII Catalan]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/to-broncos-vs-st-estevexiii-catalan-la-jeunesse-regale-arnaune/|title=TO Broncos vs St Estève-XIII Catalan – La jeunesse régale Arnauné}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
Aged just 18 years old, Sangaré made his debut<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://twitter.com/TOXIII/status/731869097550598144|title=Prop Justin Sangaré comes on for his first match}}</ref> for the first team on 15 May 2016, coming off the bench in the 46–24 win at [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://southleedslife.com/toulouse-prove-much-hunslet-hawks/|title=Rugby - Match details - Hunslet / Toulouse Olymp. XIII - 15/05/2016 - teams - Kingstone Press League 1|website=www.southleedslife.com}}</ref> in [[2016 League 1|Kingstone Press League 1]].

On 15 September 2017, Toulouse announced that Sangaré had extended his contract until the end of the 2018 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/le-to-assure-lavenir-en-conservant-justin-sangare/|title=TO secure the future by keeping Justin Sangare|website=www.to13.com}}</ref>

Toulouse announced on 3 September 2020 that Sangaré had signed a new contract with Toulouse for the [[2021 RFL Championship]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toulouse-re-sign-justin-sangare/|title=Toulouse re-sign Justin Sangaré|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref> He subsequently played in 14 of the 15 matches in 2021, including the [[Million Pound Game]] which saw Toulouse earn promotion to [[Super League]] for 2022.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58824126|title=Match Report & line-ups|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

On 28 October 2021, Toulouse announced that Sangaré had signed a new contract that would keep him at the club until the end of the 2022 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/justin-sangare-reste-au-to-xiii/|title=Justin SANGARE reste au TO XIII/|date=October 31, 2021}}</ref> Sangaré revealed that he chose to remain with Toulouse despite offers from other [[Super League]] clubs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/toulouse-powerhouse-prop-justin-sangare-reveals-super-league-clubs-chased-him-44835/|title=Justin Sangare reveals Super League clubs chased him|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}}</ref>
On 19 July 2022, he signed a two-year deal to join [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] starting in 2023.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-confirm-signing-of-toulouse-powerhouse-justin-sangare/|title=Leeds confirm signing of Toulouse powerhouse Justin Sangare|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos===
In round 1 of the 2023 Super League season, Sangaré made his club debut for Leeds in their 42-10 loss against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/warrington-vs-leeds/58253/|title=Super League: Seven-try Warrington Wolves storm to 42-10 opening-night win over Leeds Rhinos|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
Sangare played 17 matches for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-2023-season-review-picking-out-the-best-and-worst-players-performances-results-4355920/|title=Leeds Rhinos' 2023 season review: picking out the best and worst players, performances, results|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>
He played 23 games for Leeds in the 2024 Super League which saw the club finish 8th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 12 November 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=12 Nov 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Ex-Leeds Rhinos prop becomes Salford Red Devils’ fifth new signing for 2025|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ex-leeds-rhinos-justin-sangare-becomes-salford-red-devils-fifth-new-signing}}</ref>
Sangare played 24 matches for Salford in the 2025 Super League season as the club endured one of their worst ever seasons on and off the field finishing bottom of the table due to severe financial problems and limited player numbers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.prolificnorth.co.uk/news/struggling-salford-red-devils-hit-with-two-point-deduction-while-club-addresses-weekend-fan-protests/|title=Salford Red Devils dealt double blow as nightmare 2025 takes another turn|website=www.prolificnorth.co.uk}}</ref>

===York Knights===
On 14 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[York Knights]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=14 Oct 2025|author=Doug Thomson|title=France forward joins York Knights in latest squad-strengthening move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/france-forward-joins-york-knights-in-latest-squad-strengthening-move/}}</ref>

==International career==
===France U18s===
Sangaré started on the bench in the 52–20 defeat to England Academy at Warrington on 27 May 2016.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37603/match-report-england-academy--france-u-|title=Match Report: England Academy 52 France U18 20}}</ref>

===France 9s===
The French squad that took part in the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]] in [[Sydney]], Australia in October 2019 included Sangaré. He missed out in the first match, a 12–8 defeat to Lebanon,<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/world-cup-9s-maarbani-douche-les-espoirs-francais/|title=Match Report and line-ups|website=www.ffr13.fr}}</ref> but played in the 23–6 victory over Wales and in the final game, a 38–4 loss to England.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/les-bleus-dominent-les-gallois-mais-chutent-devant-les-anglais/|title=Les Bleus dominent les Gallois mais chutent devant les Anglais|date=October 19, 2019}}</ref>

===France===
Serbia lost 54–2 to France in a friendly in Belgrade on 7 October 2018 which saw Sangaré make his debut for the full French national side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/rien-a-jeter-en-defense-chez-les-bleus/|title=Match report & line-ups|website=www.ffr13.fr}}</ref> The side was made up of players mostly drawn from the French domestic competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.lindependant.fr/2018/10/07/xiii-de-france-des-tricolores-trop-rapides-pour-les-serbes,4723509.php|title=France too quick for Serbia|website=www.lindependant.fr}}</ref> Sangaré was not selected for the test match against England or the three World Cup qualifiers later that autumn where France selected sides mostly made up of [[Catalans Dragons]] and Toulouse Olympique players from the English competitions.

Sangaré was part of the expanded French squad after the 2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s that played two games Down Under. He made his debut for the senior side on 25 October 2019 coming off the bench in the 62–4 defeat to the Junior Kangaroos.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/un-monde-decart/|title=Match Report & line-ups|website=www.ffr13.fr}}</ref> He once again came off the bench in the final match of the tour against the Western Rams five days later, which France won 22–20.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/la-solidarite-de-la-france-a-fini-par-payer/|title=La solidarité de la France a fini par payer|date=October 30, 2019}}</ref>

On 23 October 2021 Sangaré scored a try in the 10–30 test match defeat to England in Perpignan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3744700|title=France v England - Rugby League|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
